title: PubSub: no root span created when inbound message has no traceparent (breaks observability for DLQ re-queues / external publishers)
body:
## In what area(s)?

/area runtime

## Expected Behavior

When the Dapr sidecar delivers a pub/sub message to a subscriber and the inbound message carries **no** W3C trace context (no `traceparent` in metadata, no `traceparent`/`traceid` in the CloudEvent envelope), Dapr should still start a **new root span** for the subscription delivery — named `pubsub/<topic>` — and inject it into the gRPC metadata passed to the app. This gives every delivered message an end-to-end trace, regardless of whether the producer set trace headers.

## Actual Behavior

In [`pkg/runtime/pubsub/subscriptions.go:389-408`](https://github.com/dapr/dapr/blob/master/pkg/runtime/pubsub/subscriptions.go#L389-L408), span creation is gated on the presence of an inbound trace ID:

```go
iTraceID := cloudEvent[contribpubsub.TraceParentField]
if iTraceID == nil {
    iTraceID = cloudEvent[contribpubsub.TraceIDField]
}

if iTraceID != nil {                                                 // <-- gate
    if traceID, ok := iTraceID.(string); ok {
        sc, _ := diag.SpanContextFromW3CString(traceID)              // <-- swallows parse error
        spanName := "pubsub/" + msg.Topic
        ctx, span = diag.StartInternalCallbackSpan(ctx, spanName, sc, tracingSpec)
        if span != nil {
            ctx = diag.SpanContextToGRPCMetadata(ctx, span.SpanContext())
        }
    } else {
        log.Warnf("ignored non-string traceid value: %v", iTraceID)
    }
}
```

When `iTraceID` is `nil`:
- No `pubsub/<topic>` span is started.
- No trace context is injected into gRPC metadata, so the application receives no `grpc-trace-bin` / `traceparent` header.
- The application's OTel SDK has no parent to continue from. From the user's vantage point the Dapr "root span is missing" and downstream requests appear to share whatever ambient/static context the app SDK falls back to.

Secondary issues observed while reviewing:
- [`subscriptions.go:396`](https://github.com/dapr/dapr/blob/master/pkg/runtime/pubsub/subscriptions.go#L396) silently discards `SpanContextFromW3CString` parse errors — a malformed `traceparent` produces an invalid `SpanContext` and the code continues as if it were valid.
- [`pkg/runtime/subscription/subscription.go:348`](https://github.com/dapr/dapr/blob/master/pkg/runtime/subscription/subscription.go#L348) seeds the resiliency runner with `context.Background()`, severing cancellation/deadline (and any span) propagation from the outer handler `ctx`. Not the proximal cause of this bug, but worth fixing in the same area.

## Steps to Reproduce the Problem

Setup: any pub/sub broker (reproduced with Azure Service Bus, but the runtime code path is broker-agnostic) with tracing enabled (`samplingRate: "1"`).

1. Publish a message to a topic **without** any W3C trace headers in metadata or in the CloudEvent envelope. 
   - **Manual DLQ re-queue on Azure Service Bus** — using the Azure portal / CLI / Service Bus Explorer to move messages from the DLQ back to the active queue drops the AMQP application properties that carried `traceparent`. This is how the issue was originally hit in production.
   - Publishing directly to the broker (bypassing Dapr's publish path) without setting `traceparent`.
   - Setting `rawPayload=true` on the subscriber and publishing a raw payload with no trace metadata.
2. Observe in your tracing backend (Tempo, Jaeger, etc.) that **no `pubsub/<topic>` span is emitted** for the delivery, and the application-side span (if any) has no parent linking it back to the sidecar.

Expected: a fresh root `pubsub/<topic>` span per delivery; app-side spans become its children.

## Release Note

RELEASE NOTE: **FIX** PubSub subscription delivery now starts a new root span when the inbound message has no `traceparent`, so externally-published or manually re-queued (DLQ) messages remain traceable end-to-end.
